_comment: Codex CLI hooks adapter for Wingman's git-push safety gate. Verification status: authored, unverified -- no live Codex CLI install in the Wingman dev sandbox. Schema per https://learn.chatgpt.com/docs/hooks, OpenAI's official Codex hooks reference (confirmed 2026-07-22, redirected from developers.openai.com/codex/hooks): PreToolUse/PostToolUse officially intercept 'Bash, file edits performed through apply_patch, MCP tool calls, and other local function tools' -- so the Bash matcher below is now a CONFIRMED tool name, not a guess. That same source also confirms matcher values 'apply_patch', 'Edit', or 'Write' are all valid for the file-edit hook path (the hook input's own tool_name field always reports 'apply_patch' regardless of which matcher value is used) -- so the earlier blocker ('Codex's tool-name strings for file edits weren't confirmed') is resolved. Still NOT wired here: secret-guard.mjs's actual payload scan needs the JSON field name(s) inside apply_patch's tool_input (equivalent to Claude Code's toolInput.content/new_string) to know what to scan for secrets -- that specific field name is not stated in the fetched docs, so wiring it now would mean guessing the one thing this project's own culture treats as worse than not porting at all (a hook that runs but silently never actually catches anything). A live Codex CLI session's own hook/audit log (via `/hooks`) would confirm the field name in one inspection -- flagged as the concrete next step, not attempted here. Also unresolved: whether hooks are enabled by default (official docs say yes, with `[features] hooks = false` to disable, `codex_hooks` as a deprecated alias) or require opt-in (a third-party cheatsheet claimed `codex_hooks = true` opt-in with silent no-ops otherwise) -- the two sources disagree; official docs are trusted here but this hasn't been confirmed against a live install either. Deliberately NOT included: the ExitPlanMode-matcher boardroom-checkpoint gate (Codex CLI has no plan-mode tool at all; it uses approval_policy instead -- there is nothing to gate here, not a missed port).

PreToolUse._matcher_comment: CONFIRMED (2026-07-22) via OpenAI's official Codex hooks docs (learn.chatgpt.com/docs/hooks): 'Bash' is a real, documented intercepted tool for PreToolUse/PostToolUse. Previously flagged here as an unconfirmed assumption -- upgraded to confirmed by direct research, not a live install test.

hooks._command_comment: Replace <PATH-TO-YOUR-INSTALLED-WINGMAN-PLUGIN> with wherever Claude Code's plugin marketplace installed Wingman on this machine (or a checked-out copy of the LabLaunchPad/Wingman repo). Reuses the real script unmodified -- see plugins/wingman/scripts/dod-pre-push-check.mjs, which already runs standalone with zero Claude Code involvement. Simpler and more robust than this hooks.json path: install it as a real git pre-push hook instead (item 3 of the harness-adapters README) -- that fires under any harness, including this one, with no Codex-specific wiring at all.
hooks.statusMessage: Wingman: checking Boardroom verdict, tests, and threat register before allowing a git push
